summary: Backend: All 13 pytest tests in /app/backend/tests/test_aasha_api.py PASSED (100%). Installed missing deps (websockets, pytest-asyncio). Frontend: Auth (signup→/chat redirect), sidebar, btn-new menu, dialog-new-chat, user listing — ALL WORK. CRITICAL BUG: clicking a user in New Chat dialog crashes the app with React 'Maximum update depth exceeded' (infinite render loop), so DM never opens, message send & attach menu & bubble dropdown could NOT be tested.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: 'Maximum update depth exceeded' infinite loop the moment an active conversation is selected. Root cause: in /app/frontend/src/pages/ChatPage.jsx:138 the prop `registerMessageHandler={(fn) => setMessageHandler(() => fn)}` is a NEW function reference on every render. ChatWindow's useEffect (lines 31-42) has `registerMessageHandler` in its dependency array, so it re-runs every render → calls back into parent setState → ChatPage re-renders → new fn ref → loop. Fix: wrap with useCallback in ChatPage (or omit registerMessageHandler from ChatWindow's deps and use a ref). Crash blocks: opening any DM, sending text, attach menu, message bubble dropdown.

integration_issues.flow: Open DM after selecting user from New Chat
integration_issues.issue: Backend POST /api/conversations succeeds (verified by backend tests), but UI crashes before chat-window mounts due to render loop above.

action_items: Fix ChatPage.jsx:138 infinite render loop. Recommended: const handleRegister = useCallback((fn) => setMessageHandler(() => fn), []); then registerMessageHandler={handleRegister}. Alternative: store handler in a ref inside ChatPage and pass a stable callback.
After fix, retest: open DM with Alice, send text, verify attach menu (image/file/location), verify message-bubble Forward/Share dropdown.
critical_code_review_comments: ChatPage.jsx passes inline arrow as registerMessageHandler — unstable reference causes infinite loop with ChatWindow's useEffect dependency.
ChatWindow.jsx useEffect at line 31 depends on registerMessageHandler — should either receive a stable callback (useCallback) or use a ref-based pattern.
Consider using a single global socket message dispatcher (e.g., context/useSyncExternalStore) instead of parent-managed handler swap to avoid this class of bug.

success_rate.frontend: ~50% (auth + sidebar + new-chat dialog + user list verified; chat-window/messaging blocked by render-loop crash)

test_credentials: alice@example.com / password123, bob@example.com / password123 (existing). Created during test: e2e_<timestamp>@example.com / password123 (signup verified working).
seed_data_creation: Each Playwright run creates a fresh user e2e_<timestamp>@example.com via /api/auth/register; not cleaned up (acceptable for preview env).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite (/app/backend/tests/test_aasha_api.py) is fully green — 13/13 passing. Don't reinstall deps. The blocking issue is purely frontend: render loop in ChatPage when active conversation is set. Once E1 fixes ChatPage.jsx:138 (useCallback), continue with: send text via message-input/btn-send, expand btn-attach (image/file/location), hover on bubble msg-* and click its dropdown trigger to verify Forward + Share. Test users Alice & Bob already exist; their data-testid in dialog list looks like user-<uuid>.
rca of the issue: ChatPage.jsx line 138: registerMessageHandler={(fn) => setMessageHandler(() => fn)} → new fn ref every render. ChatWindow.jsx line 42: useEffect deps [registerMessageHandler, conversation.id] → effect re-runs → calls registerMessageHandler(fn) → parent setMessageHandler() → parent re-renders → new prop ref → infinite loop. Fix with useCallback in parent, or use a ref-based handler registry.
